openmeter/billing/charges/worker.purpose: Organisational parent for charge-advancement workers. Splits the advance concern into two execution models: batch synchronous (advance/) for scheduled sweeps across all customers, and event-driven asynchronous (asyncadvance/) for single-customer advances triggered by a Watermill/event-bus message. Both children share the same constraint: all charge mutations must flow through charges.ChargeService, never through Ent adapters directly.

patterns.name: Config struct + constructor validation
patterns.description: Each child defines a Config struct with required fields and validates them in the constructor before returning the concrete type. advance/ validates inline in NewAdvancer; asyncadvance/ delegates to Config.Validate().
patterns.example: func NewAdvancer(config Config) (*AutoAdvancer, error) { if config.ChargesService == nil { return nil, fmt.Errorf(...) } }

patterns.name: Service-only access — no direct Ent calls
patterns.description: Both advance.AutoAdvancer and asyncadvance.Handler hold only a charges.ChargeService interface. Ent/adapter code is never imported or called in these packages.
patterns.example: type AutoAdvancer struct { chargesService charges.ChargeService; logger *slog.Logger }

patterns.name: Error accumulation in batch paths
patterns.description: advance.All collects per-customer errors into []error and returns errors.Join — one failing customer never blocks others. asyncadvance.Handle returns the error directly (single-customer scope).
patterns.example: var errs []error; for _, cust := range customers { if err := a.AdvanceCharges(ctx, cust); err != nil { errs = append(errs, ...) } }; return errors.Join(errs...)

patterns.name: Pagination via CollectAll
patterns.description: advance.ListCustomersToAdvance uses pagination.CollectAll with a pagination.NewPaginator closure so the full customer list is fetched page-by-page without manual cursor management.
patterns.example: return pagination.CollectAll(ctx, pagination.NewPaginator(func(ctx context.Context, page pagination.Page) (pagination.Result[customer.CustomerID], error) { return a.chargesService.ListCustomersToAdvance(ctx, ...) }), defaultPageSize)

patterns.name: Structured logging with slog
patterns.description: Both packages use log/slog with context-aware methods (InfoContext, DebugContext, WarnContext, ErrorContext) and named key-value pairs for namespace and customer_id.
patterns.example: h.logger.WarnContext(ctx, "failed to advance charges", slog.String("namespace", event.Namespace), slog.String("customer_id", event.CustomerID))

patterns.name: Single-responsibility Handle method in async handler
patterns.description: asyncadvance.Handler exposes exactly one public method: Handle(ctx, *charges.AdvanceChargesEvent) error. Batch/pagination logic must not be added here.
patterns.example: func (h *Handler) Handle(ctx context.Context, event *charges.AdvanceChargesEvent) error { _, err := h.chargesService.AdvanceCharges(ctx, charges.AdvanceChargesInput{Customer: customer.CustomerID{Namespace: event.Namespace, ID: event.CustomerID}}); ... }

key_file_guides.role: Batch sweeper: lists all customers with eligible charges via paginated service call, then advances each; accumulates errors across customers.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Time is captured once at the top of All() via time.Now() and passed to ListCustomersToAdvance — do not move it inside the paginator closure or per-customer calls.

key_file_guides.role: Single-customer event handler wired as a Watermill message consumer; constructs customer.CustomerID from event fields and calls AdvanceCharges.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Returns the error from AdvanceCharges — do NOT return nil to suppress retries; Watermill uses the error for retry/dead-letter routing.

anti_patterns: Calling Ent/adapter code directly — all mutations must go through charges.ChargeService
Returning on first customer error in batch paths — must accumulate with errors.Join
Introducing context.Background() or context.TODO() — always propagate caller's ctx
Adding batch/pagination logic to asyncadvance.Handler — that belongs in advance.AutoAdvancer
Niling out the error return in asyncadvance.Handle to silently swallow failures

decisions.decision: Separate packages for batch (advance) and async (asyncadvance) advance paths
decisions.rationale: Execution models differ — batch sweeps all customers with pagination and error accumulation; async handles a single Watermill event with pass-through error semantics. Mixing them would couple scheduling and event-bus concerns.

decisions.decision: Error accumulation via errors.Join in the batch path instead of early return
decisions.rationale: One failing customer must not prevent others from being advanced; all failures are surfaced together for observability.

code_examples.scenario: Constructing and running the batch sweeper in a scheduled job
